data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Sees $228 Million in Liquidations Over 24 Hours
data.description: According to BlockBeats, data from Coinglass reveals that the cryptocurrency market experienced liquidations totaling $228 million in the past 24 hours. A total of 86,742 traders were affected globally, with long positions accounting for $79.64 million and short positions for $148 million. Ethereum (ETH) recorded the highest liquidation amount among cryptocurrencies, with $95.32 million.

data.title: Montenegro's Former Justice Minister Proposes €500 Million Bond for Bitcoin Reserve
data.description: According to Foresight News, Montenegro's former Justice Minister has put forward a proposal to issue a €500 million bond aimed at establishing a national Bitcoin strategic reserve. This initiative seeks to bolster the country's financial stability and leverage the growing influence of digital currencies in the global economy. The proposal highlights the potential benefits of integrating Bitcoin into national reserves, reflecting a broader trend of countries exploring cryptocurrency as a viable asset class. Montenegro's move could position it as a pioneer in adopting digital currency strategies at a national level.

data.title: Eric Trump Warns Against Shorting Bitcoin and Ethereum
data.description: According to Foresight News, Eric Trump, the second son of U.S. President Donald Trump, recently posted on X, advising against shorting Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH). He cautioned that those who do so may face significant losses.

data.title: Fidelity Sees Significant Inflows in FETH and FBTC
data.description: According to BlockBeats, recent data from Farside Investors indicates that Fidelity's FETH experienced a net inflow of $132 million, while FBTC saw a net inflow of $30.5 million. These substantial inflows highlight growing investor interest in these financial products.

data.title: World Liberty Financial Seeks to Establish Public Company for Token Holdings
data.description: According to BlockBeats, World Liberty Financial (WLFI), backed by the Trump family, is in discussions with major investors to form a publicly listed company to hold its WLFI tokens. This move aims to capitalize on the growing trend of digital asset reserve companies. Sources familiar with the matter indicate that the deal's structure is still being finalized, with a fundraising target of approximately $1.5 billion. Significant investors from the technology and cryptocurrency sectors have been approached to participate in this initiative.

data.title: REX-Osprey SOL ETF Sees Significant Inflows
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Farside Investors reported that the REX-Osprey SOL spot ETF experienced a net inflow of $6.4 million yesterday. Since its launch, the ETF has accumulated a total net inflow of $141 million.

data.title: Trump-Backed World Liberty Financial Plans $1.5 Billion Token Launch
data.description:

source: https://coincu.com/353523-trump-family-wlfi-token-launch
